name: ACKS Imperial Imprint — Core Rulebook
_doc: Reglas y tablas comunes a todas las campañas ACKS. Importado del acks-assistant (Marcos). Las campañas ACKS referencian este rulebook con `referenceRulebook: 'acks_core'`.

expedition._doc: Variable mi/día por terrain. Ver wilderness.expedition_rates

wilderness._doc: ACKS JJ Ch.2 Section B — Wilderness Adventures

Climate Zones (Koppen): Tropical savanna with a dry winter
Tropical Savanna with a dry summer
Temperate with a dry hot summer
Temperate with a dry warm summer
Temperate with a dry cold summer
Temperate with a dry winter and hot summer
Temperate with a dry winter and warm summer
Temperate with a dry winter and cold summer
Temperate and damp year-round with a hot summer
Temperate and damp year-round with a warm summer
Temperate and damp year-round with a cold summer
Continental with a hot dry summer
Continental with a warm dry summer
Continental with a cold dry summer
Continental with a dry summer and very cold winter
Continental with a dry winter and hot summer
Continental with a dry winter and warm summer
Continental with a dry winter and cold summer
Continental with a dry winter and very cold winter
Continental and damp year-round with a hot summer
Continental and damp year-round with a warm summer
Continental and damp year-round with a cold summer
Continental and damp year-round with a very cold winter

dungeon._doc: ACKS JJ Ch.2 Section A — Dungeon Adventures

rest_requirement._doc: 1 turn rest cada 6 turnos activos. Sin rest = fatigue.

Safe Haven (1d10): A talus cave on a rocky slope.
A forested dell hidden by fells that flank it on either side.
A rocky ledge inaccessible except by one easily-guarded route.
A meadow surrounded by a nigh-impenetrable thicket of thorny briar.
A clearing concealed by a dense grove of trees.
A deep gully with high sides.
A narrow crevice with rocky overhangs.
An abandoned cabin, hut, or tree house.
An abandoned warren with underground tunnels dug by giant varmints.
A limestone cave with a grotto.
Obstacle Types: Precarious ledge along a cliff face
Rocky, scrambling ascent up a hill
Sheer ascent of cliff or mountain
Poison Types (1d20): 2d10 doses of giant killer bee venom
3d6 doses of giant black widow venom
Curse Types (1d10): Infectious:  The curse is infectious in some fashion.
Transformational: The curse transforms its victims.
Intermittent: The curse applies only at specific times.
Devastating: The effect of the curse is greater than normal.
Horrifying: The curse makes the victim fearsome to their allies.
Confusing: The curse affects the victim's thought patterns.
Forgetful: The curse affects the victim's memory.
Enfeebling: The curse affects the victim's physical attributes.
Locational: The curse affects the location, rather than specific victims.
Object: The curse is tied to an object, and affects its bearers or wielders.
Complex Map Power (1d12): Roll again on the Major Power Table

settlements._doc: ACKS Rulebook Ch.8 — Visiting Settlements. Market Class determines availability, services, encounters.
settlements._settlement_encounters_doc: ACKS RB Ch.8 Visiting Settlements section has hourly encounter checks by district (Avenue/Slums/Temple District/etc.)

mercantile._doc: ACKS Rulebook Ch.8 — Mercantile Ventures. Trade routes between settlements. Supply & Demand drives prices.
process: 1. Buy goods at Class A market (baseline_price)
2. Travel to Class B market (consume Supply, encounter checks)

modifiers: +1 if destination is Class I or II
+1 if Class A goods to Class B markets
-1 if Class B goods returning to Class A
+1 per neighboring market with shortage of this good

_links_to_data._doc: Estos JSONs son demasiado grandes para inlinear (1MB+). El cockpit V5C los carga vía fetch cuando necesita.
